title: leontopodium ochroleucum subsp. conglobatum
intro: Leontopodium ochroleucum subsp. conglobatum, also known as the Alpine Edelweiss, is a subspecies of the Edelweiss plant, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Leontopodium ochroleucum subsp. conglobatum include reducing inflammation and improving breathing, as well as its potential use in treating allergies and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its flowers can also be dried and used as a natural remedy for respiratory issues.
